description: UC6 negation probe — RED case documenting the dominant failure surface USERS.md UC6 already names. Patient 90006's note says 'Patient denies penicillin allergy. NKDA on intake form.' — the keyword (penicillin) and marker (allergy) co-occur, so the rule fires today. Correct semantics is no flag; this case asserts the correct behavior, so it ships RED until narrative_only_allergy gains a negation filter (NKDA / 'denies' / 'no known drug allergies' suppression). When that lands the case flips green; codifying current-broken-behavior as expected would hide the gap.
query: Are there any allergy reconciliation issues to flag for this patient?
